advantages: One-hot encoding avoids false ordering and gives linear models an interpretable coefficient per category level.
Ordinal encoding is compact and appropriate when levels have a defensible rank (survey scales, size grades).
Target encoding compresses high-cardinality fields into a single numeric column, reducing memory versus thousands of sparse dummies.
Fitting encoders inside sklearn Pipelines keeps train and inference mappings consistent and versioned with the model.
disadvantages: One-hot expansion blows up feature count and sparsity when cardinality is large, increasing overfitting and training cost.
Ordinal integers on nominal data mislead linear and distance-based models into treating labels as equally spaced numbers.
Target encoding without out-of-fold estimation leaks labels and produces unrealistically strong offline validation scores.
Unseen categories at serving time require explicit handling—ignore, unknown bucket, or fallback—or predictions become biased or fail.
